title: Fenced Garden Enclosure with Raised Bed
summary: A framed 4x4-post enclosure wrapped in chicken wire with a hinged gate, containing a built-in raised garden bed — animal-proof fencing around planting space, not just a bed.
description:
This project fences a raised garden bed against animals rather than just building the bed itself — 4x4 corner posts framed with 2x4 and 2x6 rails, wrapped in chicken wire from the ground up, with a full hinged door for human access. Build the two side walls first and square each one by measuring both diagonals — do this before the enclosure ever gets set in its final position, since a side wall is far easier to true up flat on the ground than after it's standing.

**The front and back wall framing gets built and squared, and the whole enclosure gets set in its final position at that stage** — the plan is explicit that this happens mid-build, not at the very end, since the remaining steps (wire, raised bed boards, door) all reference the enclosure's actual final position and level.

Wrap the bottom four feet in chicken wire or mesh, leaving the door opening clear, then cover the exposed wire edges with an additional row of trim boards — raw wire edges left exposed are both unsightly and a scratch hazard. The raised bed boards attach to the inside of the frame once the enclosure is square and wired. **The door gets its own wire-wrapped frame**, built, squared, and wire-covered exactly like the main walls before being hung with gate hinges — treat it as a small version of the same wall assembly, not an afterthought.

tools.note: Trimming door frame ends to fit.

materials.name: Chicken wire or mesh, 48 in wide

cutList.note: Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° off square, not parallel.
Cut to fit, marked and trimmed after dry-fitting over the door frame.

steps.title: Build the side walls
steps.body: On a flat, level surface, lay the 4x4 posts out and attach the side wall rails to the top and bottom with 3" screws and exterior glue. Attach a center rail on edge. Check square by measuring diagonals. Build two side walls.

steps.title: Build the front and back walls
steps.body: Attach 2x4 top rails and 2x6 bottom rails to create the front and back wall framing. Check square by measuring diagonals. Set the enclosure in its final position at this point.

steps.title: Add the center rail and corner braces
steps.body: Attach the remaining center rail on edge, 24" from the 4x4 corner posts, to the front and back walls. Check the whole enclosure is square, then attach the angled corner braces to the insides.

steps.title: Wrap the enclosure in wire
steps.body: Wrap the bottom four feet of the enclosure in chicken wire or mesh, securing with staples and leaving the door opening clear. Cover the exposed wire edges with additional trim boards.
materials: Chicken wire or mesh, 48 in wide

steps.title: Build the raised bed
steps.body: Attach the raised bed side and end boards to the inside of the frame.

steps.title: Build the door
steps.body: Attach 1x2s to the top and bottom of the door frame sides, checking square. Dry-fit the cross support over the frame, mark, and trim to fit with a circular saw or jigsaw. Staple wire or mesh to the door frame.
materials: Chicken wire or mesh, 48 in wide

steps.title: Trim the door and hang it
steps.body: Attach the door trim to the frame with 2-1/2" screws. Attach the door to the enclosure with gate hinges.

unresolvedImages.alt: Fenced garden enclosure with a raised bed and gate
